title:
WHAT ARE YOU
GOING TO DO?
titlePlain: Future Plans — going to / present continuous (A2)
subtitle: Listening & Grammar · A2 · ~45 min

blocks.title: 📘 PLANES A FUTURO · going to / present continuous
blocks.intro: Dos formas de hablar del futuro: going to (planes) y el present continuous (citas con día/hora). Es la última lección antes del examen.

cells.body:
am/is/are + going to + base
I'm going to study.
We're going to launch it.

am/is/are + verbo-ing + hora
I'm meeting the client at 3.
We're flying on Monday.

Are you going to start?
I'm not going to wait.
Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.

no olvides be: I going to go
I'm going to go.
going to = plan · present cont. = cita fija

blocks.ladderLabel: Marcadores de futuro — cuándo va a pasar:
blocks.note: Cómo funciona: escucha los planes 🎧, completa 📝 y ordená 🧩. ¡Última lección del mundo A2 — después viene el examen!

blocks.title: ★ WHAT'S NEXT?
blocks.intro: El equipo mira hacia adelante: un proyecto nuevo y un viaje de trabajo. Después de esta lección viene el examen del mundo A2.

cast.sp: la team lead

blocks.title: AUDIO 1 · PLANS FOR THE NEW PROJECT
blocks.desc: Sofia y Marcos planean el próximo proyecto. Fíjate en "going to".
blocks.speakers: 🗣 Sofia + Marcos

questions.q: What are they going to build?
options: An app for a travel company

questions.q: What is Marcos going to do on the project?
They are going to have the first meeting on Monday.

speaking.prompt: Describe a plan for a project or trip. Use I'm going to… three times.
speaking.starter: We're going to create a new app. I'm going to write the code, and…

transcript.html: Marcos, we're going to start a new project next month. Are you ready?
Yes! What are we going to build?
We're going to create an app for a travel company. It's going to be big.
How are we going to organize the team?
I'm going to lead the design, and you're going to write the code.
When are we going to start?
We're going to have the first meeting on Monday. I'm going to send the plan tomorrow.

blocks.title: COMPLETÁ EL FUTURO
blocks.intro: Elegí going to o present continuous.

items.q: Next month we ___ start a new project.
I can't meet on Friday — I ___ the client at three.
She ___ run a marathon this year.
On Monday, I ___ to Madrid at nine.
I'm ___ going to use the translator so much.

blocks.title: AUDIO 2 · A WORK TRIP
blocks.desc: Marcos cuenta su agenda para un viaje. Fíjate en el present continuous con horas.

questions.q: Where is Marcos traveling next week?
What is he doing on Tuesday?
options: Meeting the client and having lunch

speaking.prompt: Describe your schedule for next week. Use the present continuous with times (I'm meeting… at…).
speaking.starter: On Monday, I'm flying to Madrid at nine. On Tuesday, I'm meeting…

transcript.html: Next week is going to be busy, because I'm traveling to Madrid for work. On Monday, I'm flying to Madrid at nine. On Tuesday, I'm meeting the client at ten, and we're having lunch at one. On Wednesday, I'm giving a presentation. I'm not working on Friday, so I'm going to relax.

blocks.title: ORDENÁ EL PLAN
blocks.intro: Tocá las palabras para formar la frase.

items.answer: We are going to launch it
She is going to run a marathon

blocks.title: ✍️ MY PLANS
blocks.intro: Escribe tus planes (5–6 frases): tus metas y tu agenda de la próxima semana.
checklist: Usa 1 cita fija con present continuous + hora (I'm meeting… at…)
Incluye 1 frase negativa (I'm not going to…)
Usa un marcador de futuro (next week, on Monday, in summer)
blocks.example: Next month, I'm going to start a new English course. I'm going to study every evening. On Tuesday, I'm meeting my teacher at five, and on Saturday, I'm taking a level test. In summer, I'm going to travel to use my English. I'm really excited!
blocks.hw: Escribe tus planes y graba un audio. Envíame ambos por WhatsApp. Después, ¡a por el examen del mundo A2!
